title: Jessie Buckley Wins Historic Best Actress Oscar for Hamnet
text: Jessie Buckley made Academy Awards history by becoming the first Irish actress to win the Best Actress Oscar at the 98th ceremony held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. She won for her role in Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao. Buckley's win capped a dominant awards season sweep, having secured all major accolades including SAG-AFTRA, BAFTA, Critics Choice, and Golden Globe awards. This was her second Oscar nomination, following a previous Best Supporting Actress nod for The Lost Daughter. During her emotional acceptance speech, Buckley thanked her fellow nominees and acknowledged her family, noting that Ireland bought them flights to attend. She expressed gratitude to her parents for encouraging them to dream and to never be defined by expectation. Addressing her husband Freddie Sorensen, she stated she loves him and called him the most incredible dad, humorously mentioning their eight-month-old daughter.
